facts.text: Lacrosse is one of the oldest team sports in North America, with origins dating back centuries among Indigenous peoples.
The Haudenosaunee (Iroquois) Confederacy originally called lacrosse 'dehuntshigwa'es,' meaning 'men hit a rounded object.'
In men's field lacrosse, each team fields 10 players: a goalie, three defensemen, three midfielders, and three attackmen.
The Premier Lacrosse League (PLL) was founded by Paul Rabil and his brother Mike in 2018.
Women's lacrosse does not allow body checking, making it a fundamentally different game from the men's version.
Box lacrosse is played indoors on a hockey rink-sized floor, with six players per side including the goalie.
Jim Brown, often called the greatest NFL running back of all time, was also an All-American lacrosse player at Syracuse University.
The Tewaaraton Award is given annually to the top male and female college lacrosse players in the United States.
Lacrosse was played at the Olympics in 1904 in St. Louis and 1908 in London before being dropped.
A standard men's lacrosse field is 110 yards long and 60 yards wide, similar in size to a football field.
The Iroquois Nationals are the only Indigenous team that competes internationally in any sport at the world championship level.
Gary Gait is considered one of the greatest lacrosse players ever, known for his 'Air Gait' move at Syracuse.
In women's lacrosse, the draw control at the start of play replaces the face-off used in men's lacrosse.
The National Lacrosse League (NLL) is North America's professional box lacrosse league, founded in 1986.
Lacrosse sticks consist of two main parts: the head (where the ball is caught and thrown) and the shaft.
The first collegiate lacrosse game was played between NYU and Manhattan College in 1877.
Paul Rabil became the first lacrosse player to be featured in an ESPN Body Issue.
Maryland women's lacrosse has won more NCAA championships than any other program in the sport.
Traditional Indigenous lacrosse games could last for days and involve fields that stretched for miles.
The fastest recorded lacrosse shot was clocked at over 116 mph by Patrick Luehrsen.
The French missionaries in the 17th century gave the sport the name 'la crosse' because the stick resembled a bishop's ceremonial staff (crosse).
Lacrosse was announced as a returning sport for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.
In box lacrosse, the shot clock is 30 seconds, making it a fast-paced, high-scoring game.
Johns Hopkins University has one of the most storied men's lacrosse programs in NCAA history with 44 national titles (including pre-NCAA era).
The lacrosse ball is made of solid rubber and is about 7.75 to 8 inches in circumference.
Mikey Powell of Syracuse won three consecutive Tewaaraton Awards from 2002 to 2004.
Women's lacrosse requires players to wear eye protection but does not mandate helmets in most levels of play.
The World Lacrosse Championship (formerly the World Lacrosse Championship) has been held since 1967.
Canada is the dominant force in international box lacrosse, having won multiple world championships.
Lacrosse is the fastest-growing sport in US high schools, with participation increasing over 500% since the mid-1990s.
In men's lacrosse, face-offs occur after every goal, at the start of each quarter, and after certain penalties.
The lacrosse crease is a circular area around each goal with a 9-foot radius in men's field lacrosse.
John Grant Jr. is one of the most prolific scorers in NLL history, earning MVP honors multiple times.
Native Americans played lacrosse as preparation for war, to resolve conflicts between tribes, and for spiritual reasons.
The PLL uses a touring model, traveling to different cities each weekend rather than having home stadiums.
Lyle Thompson, a member of the Onondaga Nation, won the Tewaaraton Award in 2015 at the University at Albany.
A goalie's lacrosse stick has a much wider head than a field player's, measuring up to 12 inches wide.
In women's lacrosse, the field has no boundaries on the sidelines in its traditional form, though modified rules now include them.
Syracuse University has won 10 NCAA Division I men's lacrosse championships.
The NCAA men's lacrosse championship game is known as 'Championship Monday' and is typically held on Memorial Day weekend.
Lacrosse was originally known as 'The Creator's Game' because the Haudenosaunee believed it was a gift from the Creator.
In box lacrosse, goalies wear equipment similar to ice hockey goalies, including large chest protectors and leg pads.
Virginia's men's lacrosse team won back-to-back NCAA championships in 2019 and 2021.
The US Lacrosse organization was founded in 1998 to govern and promote the sport at all levels.
Lacrosse helmets for men's play must have a full face mask and chin strap, meeting NOCSAE safety standards.
The first women's lacrosse game in the US was played at the Bryn Mawr School in Baltimore in 1926.
A men's lacrosse game consists of four 15-minute quarters in college and professional play.
The Iroquois Nationals won the bronze medal at the 2014 World Lacrosse Championship.
In college lacrosse, a personal foul results in either a one- or three-minute penalty.
Northwestern women's lacrosse has won multiple NCAA championships and is one of the top programs in the nation.
Lacrosse sticks are strung with mesh pockets, with traditional pockets using leather and nylon strings.
The original lacrosse balls were made from deerskin stuffed with hair or carved from wood.
Major League Lacrosse (MLL) operated from 2001 to 2020 before merging with the PLL.
The cross-checking penalty in men's lacrosse occurs when a player uses the shaft of the stick between their hands to push an opponent.
Lacrosse is Canada's official national summer sport, designated by the National Sports of Canada Act in 1994.
In women's lacrosse, the 8-meter arc around the goal is the key scoring area, similar to the crease in men's play.
The first MLL championship was won by the Long Island Lizards in 2001.
Some Indigenous lacrosse games in the 1700s involved over 1,000 players on each side.
Players in men's field lacrosse must wear helmets, shoulder pads, arm pads, and gloves.
The sport of lacrosse has been growing rapidly in non-traditional regions like Texas, Florida, and California.
Gary Gait's famous 'Air Gait' goal, scored by diving from behind the goal and stuffing the ball in midair, was first performed in a 1988 NCAA semifinal.
The face-off in men's lacrosse involves two players squatting at the center of the field with their sticks on the ground.
Women's lacrosse has different field markings, including a 12-meter fan and an 8-meter arc around the goal.
The World Lacrosse Sixes format was created to showcase the sport's Olympic potential, featuring six-on-six play on a smaller field.
In box lacrosse, the floor is typically made of artificial turf laid over a hockey rink's concrete base.
The US has won the most men's World Lacrosse Championships.
A regulation lacrosse goal measures 6 feet by 6 feet.
Many high school lacrosse programs in the Northeast US have existed for over 50 years.
Lyle and Miles Thompson, known as the 'Thompson Twins,' both starred at Albany and are members of the Onondaga Nation.
The first World Lacrosse Championship was held in Toronto in 1967.
In professional lacrosse, the two-point line extends in an arc 16 yards from the goal for long-range scoring.
Lacrosse goalies typically use sticks ranging from 40 to 72 inches long.
The Cherokee, Choctaw, and other Southeastern tribes played a lacrosse variant called 'stickball.'
Virginia became the first state to sanction girls' lacrosse as a high school sport in 1998.
In men's lacrosse, offensive players cannot enter the crease, but can reach in with their stick to score.
The NLL season runs during the winter and spring, from December through June.
Modern lacrosse pocket rules specify that the ball must be visible above the sidewall when held horizontally.
The rivalry between Johns Hopkins and Maryland is one of the oldest and most intense in college lacrosse.
US Lacrosse reports that youth lacrosse participation has grown by more than 300% since 2001.
In women's lacrosse, a free position is awarded for major fouls committed inside the critical scoring area.
The PLL introduced a two-point line to professional outdoor lacrosse in its inaugural season.
Paul Rabil scored the fastest goal in PLL history, netting one just seconds into a game.
Lacrosse was a demonstration sport at the 1928 Amsterdam Olympics and the 1932 Los Angeles Olympics.
The traditional wooden lacrosse stick, or 'woody,' has largely been replaced by modern synthetic sticks.
In box lacrosse, transition play and fast breaks are critical because of the smaller playing surface.
Maryland's men's lacrosse program won the NCAA championship in 2017, ending a title drought.
Women's lacrosse sticks have a shallower pocket than men's sticks, requiring more precise cradling technique.
The NLL's most famous franchise, the Buffalo Bandits, has one of the most loyal fanbases in indoor lacrosse.
Lacrosse is played in over 80 countries worldwide as of 2025.
In men's lacrosse, a shot on goal that goes in off a teammate is credited to the last offensive player who touched it.
The first intercollegiate lacrosse league was formed in 1882 among several Northeastern universities.
Attackmen in lacrosse typically use shorter sticks (40-42 inches) for better ball handling.
The Haudenosaunee Confederacy includes six nations: Mohawk, Oneida, Onondaga, Cayuga, Seneca, and Tuscarora.
The US women's lacrosse team has dominated the Women's Lacrosse World Championship since its inception.
In 2028, lacrosse will return to the Olympics for the first time in 120 years, using the Sixes format.
Defensemen typically use long poles (52-72 inches) to increase their reach for checks and poke checks.
The 'clear' in lacrosse refers to moving the ball from the defensive end past midfield to start the offense.
Japan has become one of the fastest-growing lacrosse nations in Asia.
In women's lacrosse, there is no shot clock at the college level, though a possession timer exists where the attack must gain possession in the offensive zone.
The annual Army-Navy lacrosse game is one of the sport's most watched regular-season events.
Lacrosse gloves for men's play feature reinforced padding on the back of the hand and fingers.
The first PLL Championship was won by the Whipsnakes LC in 2019.
In traditional Native American lacrosse, scoring a goal sometimes required carrying the ball to a rock or tree, not a netted goal.
The 'ride' in lacrosse is the offensive team's attempt to prevent the defense from clearing the ball.
Cornell University has a storied tradition in men's lacrosse, making multiple Final Four appearances.
Box lacrosse originated in Canada in the 1930s as a way to play lacrosse during the winter months.
Short-stick defensive midfielders (SSDMs) play a key role in covering opposing midfielders.
The first women's lacrosse world championship was held in 1982 in Nottingham, England.
In men's lacrosse, penalties result in the offending player sitting in a penalty box, giving the other team a man-up situation.
Lacrosse has been growing rapidly in the UK, Australia, and Israel.
The 'dodge' is a fundamental offensive move in lacrosse, used to get past a defender one-on-one.
Jim Brown scored 43 goals in his junior season at Syracuse, a remarkable achievement for any era.
The goalie in lacrosse plays a crucial role as the last line of defense and the first outlet on the clear.
Fogo (face-off, get off) specialists have become common in modern men's lacrosse, focusing only on winning face-offs.
The NLL Championship trophy is called the Champions Cup.
Youth lacrosse typically uses smaller fields and modified rules to accommodate younger players.
In women's lacrosse, the goalkeeper must stay inside the crease to receive certain protections.
The Rabil brothers raised venture capital funding to start the PLL, a first for professional lacrosse.
Ground balls are one of the most important statistics in lacrosse, often determining possession advantage.
The lacrosse midfield line divides the field in half, and each team must keep a minimum number of players on each side.
Denver's men's lacrosse program won the 2015 NCAA championship, the first title for a school west of the Mississippi.
The 'cradling' technique in lacrosse involves rotating the wrist and arm to keep the ball secure in the pocket.
Australia's men's lacrosse team has consistently finished in the top four at World Lacrosse Championships.
In men's lacrosse, a body check is legal when the opponent has the ball or is within five yards of it.
Women's lacrosse fouls are classified as major or minor, with different consequences for each.
The Toronto Rock is the most successful franchise in NLL history, with multiple Champion's Cup titles.
Lacrosse was originally played by Native Americans as a way to train warriors for battle.
The man-up offense in lacrosse is similar to a power play in hockey, providing a numerical advantage.
In NCAA Division I men's lacrosse, teams can offer up to 12.6 scholarships.
The 'V-cut' is a common off-ball movement where a player makes a sharp change of direction to get open.
The US Lacrosse Annual Participation Survey tracks the sport's growth across youth, high school, and college levels.
In women's lacrosse, the critical scoring area extends in a 15-meter semicircle from each goal.
The Georgia Swarm, originally the Minnesota Swarm, is a prominent NLL franchise.
Lacrosse nets are typically made of 5mm or 6mm nylon cord mesh.
The 'pick' or screen in lacrosse is used to free up a teammate by blocking a defender's path.
The first NCAA Division I men's lacrosse tournament was held in 1971, won by Cornell.
In box lacrosse, penalty situations can lead to 5-on-4 or even 5-on-3 power plays.
Lyle Thompson was drafted first overall in the 2015 MLL Draft by the Florida Launch.
The pocket depth of a lacrosse stick is regulated to ensure fair play and ball control.

In men's lacrosse, long-stick midfielders (LSMs) are defensive specialists who use long poles.
The field lacrosse restraining lines keep three attackmen and three defensemen on their respective sides of the field.

The Chesapeake Bayhawks won the most MLL championships before the league merged with the PLL.

The World Indoor Lacrosse Championship is held every four years for box lacrosse.
Lacrosse is the official state sport of Maryland, designated in 2004.
In men's lacrosse, a team must advance the ball past the midfield line within 20 seconds of gaining possession.
The 'skip pass' is a long pass that bypasses several defenders, often used to shift the defense.

The NLL uses a 30-second shot clock, contributing to its fast-paced, high-scoring games.
In 1867, William George Beers codified modern lacrosse rules and helped establish the sport in Canada.
Women's lacrosse allows 12 players per team on the field, compared to 10 in men's.
The 'question mark dodge' involves a player circling away from a defender in a path shaped like a question mark.
Johns Hopkins lacrosse plays their home games at Homewood Field, one of the most iconic venues in the sport.
In box lacrosse, the playing area is enclosed by boards similar to a hockey rink.

In women's lacrosse, players wear goggles, mouthguards, and soft gloves, but not helmets at most levels.

Lacrosse stick heads are made from molded plastic or composite materials and come in various shapes.

In box lacrosse, there is no offsides rule, allowing all players to roam the entire floor.

In women's lacrosse, there are specific rules about how close a defender can be when marking the ball carrier.
